ucs.title: [LLM] Turla ComRAT v4 C2 via Gmail web interface from non-browser process
ucs.description: Hunts for Turla's signature ComRAT v4 (a.k.a. Agent.BTZ successor) which uses an embedded Gecko/Chromium-like component to log into Gmail with a stolen cookie and pull commands from inbox/attachments. Triggers when a non-browser process touches mail.google.com / accounts.google.com / googleusercontent.com endpoints — a behavior almost unique to Turla among Russian APTs (ESET, 2020 ComRAT v4 report).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest="mail.google.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*.mail.google.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="accounts.google.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*.googleusercontent.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="mail-attachment.googleusercontent.com") AND All_Traffic.dest_port=443 by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.process_name All_Traffic.user host
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`
| eval is_browser=if(match(lower(process_name),"(chrome|msedge|firefox|iexplore|brave|opera|safari|edge)\.exe"),1,0)
| where is_browser=0
| join type=left host process_name [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wmiprvse.exe","svchost.exe","mshta.exe","explorer.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","taskhostw.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process | rename Processes.dest as host Processes.process_name as process_name Processes.parent_process_name as parent_process_name Processes.process as process]
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| table firstTime lastTime host src user process_name parent_process_name process dest dest_port count
ucs.kql:
let GmailC2 = dynamic(["mail.google.com","accounts.google.com","googleusercontent.com","mail-attachment.googleusercontent.com","clients.google.com"]);
let Browsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","safari.exe","edge.exe"]);
let SuspectInitiators = dynamic(["powershell.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wmiprvse.exe","svchost.exe","mshta.exe","explorer.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","taskhostw.exe","dllhost.exe"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where RemotePort == 443
| where RemoteUrl has_any (GmailC2) or RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)(mail|accounts)\.google\.com"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (Browsers)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (SuspectInitiators) or isempty(InitiatingProcessFileName)
| extend ParentChain = strcat(InitiatingProcessParentFileName, " -> ", InitiatingProcessFileName)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| summarize ConnCount=count(), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp), Urls=make_set(RemoteUrl, 25), CmdLines=make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 10) by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256
| where ConnCount >= 2
| order by ConnCount desc
ucs.rationale: Turla / Secret Blizzard / Venomous Bear is the ONLY public APT documented to weaponize the Gmail web UI for C2 — ComRAT v4 logs in with a stolen cookie via an embedded browser engine and treats the inbox as a dead-drop. Legitimate Gmail traffic comes from real browsers; svchost/rundll32/powershell hitting mail.google.com is essentially pathognomonic for ComRAT. Other RU clusters (APT28, APT29, San

ucs.title: [LLM] Turla registry-resident PowerShell loader (PowerStallion / Kazuar / IronPython staging)
ucs.description: Hunts the Turla PowerShell loader pattern publicly burned in ESET (PowerStallion, 2019) and Accenture/Mandiant (Kazuar/IronNetInjector) reports: a small PowerShell stub reads a base64+AES blob from HKCU/HKLM, base64-decodes, then reflectively loads it via [Reflection.Assembly]::Load or IEX. Persistence is COM hijack, scheduled task, or WMI sub. This precise registry-read → reflective-load chain in one command line is a Turla fingerprint.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_id Processes.process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| eval cmd_lc=lower(cmdline)
| eval reads_reg = if(match(cmd_lc,"(get-itemproperty|get-itempropertyvalue|get-childitem)\s+.*?(hkcu|hklm|hkey_current_user|hkey_local_machine|registry::|microsoft\.powershell\.core\\\\registry)"),1,0)
| eval decodes  = if(match(cmd_lc,"(frombase64string|convert\]::frombase64|gzipstream|deflatestream|aes|rijndael|tripledes|rc4)"),1,0)
| eval reflects = if(match(cmd_lc,"(\[reflection\.assembly\]::load|\[appdomain\]::currentdomain|invoke-expression|iex\s*\(|\.invoke\(|\[system\.reflection\.emit)"),1,0)
| where reads_reg=1 AND decodes=1 AND reflects=1
| eval indicators=mvappend(if(reads_reg=1,"registry_read",null()), if(decodes=1,"decode",null()), if(reflects=1,"reflective_load",null()))
| table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name parent indicators cmdline
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe")
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where cmd matches regex @"(get-itemproperty|get-itempropertyvalue|get-childitem)\s+.*(hkcu|hklm|hkey_current_user|hkey_local_machine|registry::)"
| where cmd has_any ("frombase64string", "convert]::frombase64", "gzipstream", "deflatestream", "aes", "rijndael", "tripledes", "rc4")
| where cmd has_any ("[reflection.assembly]::load", "invoke-expression", "iex(", "iex (", ".invoke(", "[appdomain]::currentdomain")
| extend SuspiciousParent = iff(InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","wmiprvse.exe","svchost.exe","schtasks.exe","taskhostw.exe","explorer.exe","dllhost.exe","cmstp.exe"), 1, 0)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where strlen(RegistryValueData) > 1024
    | where RegistryKey has_any (@"HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\", @"HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Classes\CLSID\", @"HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Classes\CLSID\", @"HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Console\", @"HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\")
    | summarize BlobWrites=count(), BlobKeys=make_set(RegistryKey, 10), MaxBlobLen=max(strlen(RegistryValueData)) by DeviceName
) on DeviceName
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, SuspiciousParent, ProcessCommandLine, BlobWrites, BlobKeys, MaxBlobLen
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Turla/Snake/Uroburos has used this exact 'big base64/encrypted blob in HKCU + tiny PowerShell decryptor + reflective .NET load' pattern across PowerStallion (ESET 2019), the Kazuar/IronPython staging chain, and the Carbon dropper. The triple-condition (registry read + crypto/base64 decode + reflective load in one PS command) is rare outside of Turla and a handful of red-team frameworks; correlatin
